fact: January 4 is exactly 13 days after the winter solstice, which means days are getting longer but you can barely tell yet!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many animals are at their fluffiest - their winter coats are at maximum thickness to survive the coldest weeks ahead.
The Earth is actually closest to the Sun around this time of year, even though it's freezing cold in many places - weird, right?
January 4 falls smack in the middle of Capricorn season, making it one of the most "goat-like" days of the year according to astrology.
In Antarctica, January 4 is peak summer and the sun never sets - penguins are having their longest party day of the year!
Tree sap is frozen solid in cold regions right now, making trees sound like wooden drums when you knock on them.
This date marks when many hibernating animals like bears are in their deepest sleep - they won't wake up for months!
January 4 is the 4th day of the year, which means there are exactly 361 days left (or 362 in leap years) - that's a lot of days to go!
In northern countries, the sun rises super late and sets super early, creating those magical orange and pink skies that last for hours.
Many evergreen trees are actually at their greenest right now because they're working extra hard to photosynthesize during the short daylight hours.
January 4 is when the Quadrantids meteor shower typically peaks, shooting up to 40 meteors per hour across the night sky!
In places with snow, this is when the snow gets that perfect crunchy texture that makes the most satisfying sound when you step on it.
